hero.hero_overview:
Mobile Legends: Bang Bang Eudora

Eudora is a mage hero who specializes in lightning-based burst damage. She can stun enemies with Ball of Lightning, deal heavy magic damage with Forked Lightning, and finish off targets using her Thunder Strike ultimate. Strength: Eudora excels in team fights, controlling multiple enemies with stuns and dealing massive burst damage, rather than being an ambush hero focused on solo kills.

Revamp Eudora Skill

Passive: Superconductor
Eudora's skills inflict Superconductor for 3 seconds on hit and can trigger additional effects against enemies affected by Superconductor.

Skill 1: Forked Lightning
Eudora casts Forked Lightning in a target direction, dealing 350 plus 150 percent total magic power magic damage to enemies in a fan-shaped area.

Additional Effect: Enemies affected by Superconductor take 190 plus 30 percent total magic power extra magic damage after a short delay.

Skill 2: Ball of Lightning
Eudora hurls an orb of lightning at a target enemy, dealing 350 plus 80 percent total magic power magic damage, stunning them for 1.2 seconds, and reducing their magic defense by 10 for 1.8 seconds.

Additional Effect: If the enemy is affected by Superconductor, the orb bounces to up to 3 nearby enemies, dealing 175 plus 40 percent total magic power magic damage and stunning them for 0.6 seconds (prioritizes enemy heroes).

Ultimate: Thunders Wrath
Eudora calls down a blast of lightning on a target enemy, dealing 480 plus 180 percent total magic power magic damage.

Additional Effect: If the enemy is affected by Superconductor, dark clouds form over their head, sending an extra lightning strike after 0.8 seconds, dealing 440 plus 150 percent total magic power magic damage to enemies below and 360 plus 150 percent total magic power magic damage to nearby enemies.
hero.hero_strength:
Eudora Strengths

Poke: Thanks to her revamp Eudora's first skill can be chained with her other abilities reducing its cooldown by one point five seconds allowing her to spam skills frequently

Additional Mobility: Activating her Superconductor passive with her first skill grants Eudora twenty five percent movement speed making her more agile in fights

AOE Stun: When enemies are clumped together and her passive is active on one of them Eudora can stun multiple targets at once making her very effective in team fights

Extra Damage: Using her ultimate Thunders Wrath on a marked enemy triggers Thunderburst after a short delay dealing additional damage to enemies

Defense Reduction: Her second skill marks enemies and reduces their defense by up to twenty five at max level amplifying the damage they take from her and her teammates

Map Control: Once Eudora reaches level four, enemy marksmen and mages will struggle to clear minion waves whenever she is off the map, giving her team an advantage in controlling the lanes.
hero.hero_weakness:
Eudora Weaknesses

Long Cooldown: Eudora's skills have very long cooldowns Her first skill at level one has a seven second cooldown which can delay or punish her laning phase

No Escape Skill: As an ambush hero Eudora has no reliable escape skill Except for Flicker when the spell is on cooldown she becomes very vulnerable

Very Squishy: Eudora is easy to kill because of her low health She is one of the mages with the lowest health in the game

cooperates.cooperate_desc:
Eudora ➔ Perfect Match ➔ Tigreal
 
Compatibility
 
Synergizing Hero: Tigreal
Star Rating: ★★★★★
 
Description: Eudora works exceptionally well with Tigreal due to their complementary crowd control and initiation capabilities. Tigreal's ability to charge into enemy teams and pull targets together with his ultimate (Implosion) creates the perfect setup for Eudora's Judgment—her area-of-effect ultimate deals massive sustained damage and stuns enemies within range. Additionally, Tigreal's 2nd skill (Sacred Hammer) can stun key targets, allowing Eudora to follow up with her Electric Arrow and Thunderstruck for full burst damage. His frontline presence also shields Eudora from assassins, letting her safely output damage from the backline.
 
.

counters.counter_desc:
Counters
 
Eudora ➔ Huge Advantage ➔ Hayabusa
 
Counter Hero: Hayabusa
Star Rating: ★★★★★
 
Description: Hayabusa is a major threat to Eudora, as his high mobility and burst damage can quickly eliminate her before she can cast her skills. His 1st skill (Shadow Step) lets him close gaps instantly, while his ultimate (Ougi: Shadow Kill) allows him to dash through targets and avoid Eudora's skill shots. Hayabusa can also use bushes to ambush her—his mobility and evasive abilities make it hard for Eudora to land her stuns or slow effects. If Hayabusa gets within range, he can burst Eudora down with his combos before she can activate Flicker or rely on her team for protection. Conversely, Eudora can counter Hayabusa by placing her Thunderstruck in his common pathing and using her ultimate to zone him out if he tries to engage.

dominants.dominant_desc:
Dominant Phase
 
Early to Mid-Game Dominance Phase
 
Early to Mid-Game Dominance Phase
 
- Laning Precautions:
- Use your 1st skill (Electric Arrow) to harass enemies while clearing minion waves. Aim to bounce it to both minions and the enemy hero for extra pressure.
- Maintain a safe distance from bushes, as mobile assassins or roamers may target you. Use your 2nd skill (Thunderstruck) to check bushes by casting it into potential hiding spots.
- Manage mana carefully. Use basic attacks on minions when possible to save skill mana for harassing or escaping ganks.
- Position near your turret until you reach level 4. Once you unlock your ultimate (Judgment), you can be more aggressive and look for gank opportunities with your jungler.
- Team Fight Strategies:
- Wait for your allies to initiate before casting your ultimate. Place it in choke points or where enemies are likely to cluster to maximize area control and damage.
- Use Flicker to reposition if enemies try to focus you, or to adjust your ultimate's placement for better coverage.
- Chain your skills: start with 2nd skill to slow enemies, follow with 1st skill to stun, then activate your ultimate for sustained damage and crowd control.
- Prioritize squishy targets like marksmen or mages. Use your passive's stacked magic penetration to burst them down quickly before they can output damage.

emblems.emblem_desc:
Explanation for Choosing This Emblem Set:
This "Burst and Sustain Mage Setup" uses the Mage Emblem with the selected talent Agility (4% extra Movement Speed), complemented by other optimized talents for the revamped 2026 Eudora.
 
The 4% movement speed boost addresses her lingering mobility gap post-revamp, helping her reposition faster to stack her passive, land bouncing 1st skill hits, and adjust placement for her lingering 2nd skill and mobile ultimate. Combined with typical Mage Emblem attributes like magic power and cooldown reduction (from additional tier talents), this setup enhances both her burst potential and battlefield sustainability—allowing her to chase down enemies, dodge skill shots more effectively, and maintain pressure across all game phases. It synergizes perfectly with her revamped kit, which rewards precise positioning and consistent skill casting to maximize damage and crowd control effects.

equips.equip_desc:
Explanation for Choosing This Build and Key Equipment:
This build is optimized for the 2026 revamped Eudora, balancing burst damage, mobility, and sustain.
 
- Arcane Boots: Core early-game choice, providing +15 magic penetration to ensure her skills deal significant damage even against tanky laners or enemies with basic magic resistance.
- Clock of Destiny: Offers +75 magic power and +500 max HP, with scaling stats that grow stronger as the game progresses. This helps her stay in fights longer to fully utilize her mobile ultimate and stacked passive effects.
- Lightning Truncheon: Adds +75 magic power and +10% movement speed. Its chain lightning passive synergizes perfectly with her 1st skill's bounce mechanic, increasing area control and consistent damage output.
- Holy Crystal: Delivers +100 magic power to maximize burst damage, letting her capitalize on the extra 15% damage from her fully stacked passive.
- Genius Wand: Provides +75 magic power and +5% movement speed, while reducing enemies' magic resistance by up to 25%. This enhances the effectiveness of her lingering 2nd skill and ultimate area damage.
- Divine Glaive: Grants +65 magic power and +10% cooldown reduction. Its stacking magic penetration pairs with her passive's penetration bonus, making her deadly against high-magic-resistance targets in late game.
 
For adjustments: Swap Divine Glaive for Radiant Armor against heavy magic damage lineups, or replace Genius Wand with Athena's Shield when facing mobile assassins.

hero.hero_overview:
Hero Overview
 
Eudora is a Mage in Mobile Legends: Bang Bang focused on Crowd Control and Magic Damage. She hails from the Magic Academy, wielding lightning-based powers to dominate battles. Her role centers on zoning enemies, locking down targets, and dealing burst magic damage, often positioned in the mid-lane to gain early gold and map control.
hero.hero_strength:
Hero Strength
 
- Laning Advantage: Her low-cooldown basic skills let her harass enemies frequently and clear minion waves quickly.
- Team Fight Impact: Ultimate ability provides strong area-of-effect crowd control and high magic damage, turning fights when enemies cluster.
- Early-Mid Game Power: Excels in skirmishes and ganks due to reliable stuns and burst damage, pressuring opponents to play defensively.
- Favorable Trait: Long skill ranges allow her to stay safe while outputting damage and controlling enemies.
hero.hero_weakness:
Hero Weakness
 
- Laning Disadvantage: Vulnerable to assassins or mobile heroes that can close gaps quickly, as she lacks mobility skills.
- Team Fight Vulnerability: If caught out of position or focused first, she can't contribute effectively and relies on allies for protection.
- Late Game Limitation: Magic resistance items built by enemies can reduce her burst damage potential significantly.
- Unfavorable Trait: High mana consumption requires strict resource management, and skill casting delays make them dodgeable by experienced opponents.

hero.hero_overview:
In the official Mobile Legends: Bang Bang hero release order, Eudora is Hero #15. She was one of the original heroes released in 2016, following Rafaela (#14) and preceding Zilong (#16). Despite her low hero number and age, her recent revamp has ensured she remains a top-tier threat in the 2026 meta. 

Eudora is a Burst Mage specializing in high single-target damage and chain crowd control (CC).


[ HERO SKILLS ]
⚡Passive: Superconductor
Her skills apply a Superconductor mark to enemies (non-minions) for 3 seconds. Marked enemies take additional damage and trigger special effects when hit by her other skills.

⚡SKILL 1:  Forked Lightning (AoE/Poke)
- Eudora casts lightning in a fan-shaped area.
Revamp Perk: If the target is marked with Superconductor, a lightning chain links Eudora to the target. This deals damage over time and provides Eudora with a Movement Speed boost, helping her kite or chase.

⚡SKILL 2 : Ball Lightning (CC/Defense Reduction)
- Launches a lightning orb that stuns the target and reduces their Magic Defense.
Revamp Perk: If the target is marked with Superconductor, the orb bounces to up to 3 nearby enemies, stunning them all and reducing their defense simultaneously.

⚡SKILL 3 : Thunder's Wrath (Burst)
- Calls down a massive strike on a target area.
Revamp Perk: If the target is marked, a second Thunderburst strike follows after a short delay, dealing heavy AoE damage to the target and surrounding enemies.

[ HERO COMBOS ]
⚡The "Classic" One-Shot (2-3-1):
- Skill 2 (Stun/Mark) > Ultimate (Huge Burst) > Skill 1 (Finish & Speed boost to escape)

⚡Team Fight Chain (1-2-3)
- Skill 1 (Mark multiple enemies) > Skill 2 (Bounces to stun everyone) > Ultimate (Wipe the group).

⚡The Catch (Ult-2-1):
- Ultimate (Zoning/Mark) > Skill 2 (Stun) > Skill 1 (Chain damage).
hero.hero_strength:
⚡Enhanced Mobility: Unlike the old Eudora, the revamp's Skill 1 gives her a speed boost, making her harder to catch.

⚡Multi-Target CC: Her Skill 2 can now stun almost an entire team if they are clumped and marked.

⚡Defense Shred: Built-in Magic Defense reduction makes her lethal even against tanks.
hero.hero_weakness:
- Extremely Squishy: Lowest HP pool in the game; one mistake is usually fatal.

- Range Issues: She is a mid-range mage. If she can't reach you with Skill 2, she is vulnerable.

- Skill Reliant: Once her skills are on cooldown, her basic attacks are negligible.

equips.equip_desc: The best item build for Eudora focuses on high magic penetration and burst damage to maximize her new teamfight potential. hero.hero_overview: Known as the "Lightning Weaver," the revamped Eudora is a powerful burst Mage who has evolved from a single-target "bush camper" into a versatile teamfight threat with enhanced Area of Effect (AoE) capabilities. Her story follows her journey as a highly gifted Light Elf who once struggled to control her volatile magic due to the trauma of childhood prejudice, but eventually found her purpose at the Magic Academy under her mentor, Theresa. Now, having embraced her true identity and cut her hair as a symbol of her resolve, she serves as a master instructor who dominates the battlefield by applying her Superconductor passive to chain lightning between enemies, stun entire groups, and call down massive electrical storms.
hero.hero_strength: Eudora is a powerful mage who uses lightning to damage many enemies at once. Her main strength is her "Superconductor" ability, which allows her spells to bounce between targets and stun several people at the same time. She can quickly kill weak heroes with her high burst damage and now moves faster when using her skills, making her harder to catch. By hitting groups of enemies, she can also reduce her own cooldowns to cast spells more often during a fight. This makes her much better in team battles than the older version of the hero.
hero.hero_weakness: Eudora's main weakness is that she is very "squishy," meaning she has low health and can be killed quickly if an enemy gets close to her. While she can move a bit faster now, she still lacks a real dash or escape skill to jump over walls and run away from danger. She also relies heavily on her skills to do damage, so if her spells are on cooldown or if she misses her stun, she becomes an easy target for assassins. Additionally, she uses a lot of mana early in the game, which might force her to go back to the base often if she casts too many spells.

spell.spell_desc: Flicker is the best battle spell for the revamped Eudora because it helps her overcome her lack of escape skills and poor mobility. If you want to play more aggressively, Flameshot is a great alternative for knocking back enemies who get too close or for finishing off low-health targets from a long distance

equips.equip_desc: The Sentinel Build transforms Eudora from a glass cannon into a Tactical Disruptor, prioritizing utility and map control over raw burst. By utilizing Rapid Boots with Dire Hit, you gain the rotation speed needed to gank lanes instantly, while Glowing Wand ensures your lightning chains apply constant anti-heal and burn to the entire enemy team. Fleeting Time is the heart of this build, significantly reducing your Ultimate's cooldown so you can provide massive CC in every single skirmish. Finally, defensive items like Winter Crown and Immortality allow you to play aggressively, baiting out enemy ultimates and surviving long enough to land a second round of stuns. It's the ultimate high-IQ setup for players who want to carry the game through vision, speed, and endless crowd control.

cooperates.cooperate_desc: Tigreal is one of the best frontline partners for Eudora because of his reliable crowd control and strong initiation tools. His ability to pull and stun multiple enemies creates the perfect setup for Eudora to land her full burst combo without risk. When Tigreal successfully groups enemies, Eudora can immediately follow up with area damage and stun, turning team fights heavily in their favor. Tigreal also absorbs pressure and protects Eudora from assassins, allowing her to position safely and wait for the right moment to strike.

Khufra synergizes extremely well with Eudora due to his anti mobility crowd control and lockdown potential. His skills prevent enemies from dashing or escaping, which makes it much easier for Eudora to land her stun and burst damage. Once Khufra initiates, Eudora can freely cast her combo on immobilized targets, often deleting key damage dealers before fights even begin. This pairing is especially strong against mobile heroes that normally counter burst mages.

Atlas provides powerful area control that perfectly complements Eudora's burst focused kit. His ultimate can pull multiple enemies into a single location, creating an ideal situation for Eudora to deal massive damage and apply crowd control to several targets at once. Atlas also forces enemies to group or reposition awkwardly, which increases Eudora's chances of landing decisive combos. Together, they excel at turning team fights around with one well timed engage.

counters.counter_desc: Fanny is a strong counter to Eudora because her high mobility allows her to bypass skill range and avoid stuns. She can dive onto Eudora quickly, forcing her to use Flicker defensively or get eliminated before casting a full combo. Eudora struggles against fast assassins like Fanny since she cannot reposition quickly on her own.

Chou can punish Eudora's positioning mistakes with his crowd control and displacement skills. His Jeet Kune Do or ultimate can isolate Eudora from her team, interrupt her combo, and prevent her from applying her burst effectively. Chou's lockdown makes diving onto Eudora relatively safe for him while leaving her vulnerable.

Ling counters Eudora by exploiting her lack of mobility. He can use walls and terrain to jump around and attack her from unexpected angles, making it difficult for Eudora to land her stun or full combo. Ling's burst can eliminate her before she can react, and his constant mobility keeps him out of reach of her limited range.

dominants.dominant_desc:
- Always hide in fog of war before engaging
Eudora is strongest when enemies do not see her coming. Stay in bushes or behind walls to surprise targets with your stun and burst combo.

- Prioritize landing your stun first
Your entire combo depends on crowd control. Make sure your stun connects before committing all your damage to avoid wasting skills.

- Target squishy heroes first
Focus on marksmen, mages, and assassins. Eliminating these heroes early gives your team a huge advantage in fights and objectives.

- Watch enemy cooldowns
Engage only after enemy dash or immunity skills are used. This increases your chance of successfully bursting them down.

- Do not overstay after using combo
Once your skills are on cooldown, immediately reposition or retreat. Eudora is very vulnerable without abilities available.

- Use terrain to your advantage
Cast skills from behind walls or tight spaces to reduce the chance of being flanked or jumped by enemy divers.

Strategies by Phase

Early Game
Focus on clearing the mid lane safely while poking enemies with your skills. Avoid unnecessary risks and prioritize reaching level four as quickly as possible. Once your ultimate is available, start looking for pick offs on overextended side lane enemies. Maintain vision control around the river to support rotations and protect yourself from early ganks.

Mid Game
This is where Eudora becomes most threatening. Rotate frequently with your team and look for isolated targets before major objectives such as Turtle or turrets. Use bushes and fog of war to surprise enemies with your stun and full burst combo. Stay behind your frontline during skirmishes and only commit when you are confident your combo will secure a kill.

Late Game
Positioning becomes extremely critical in the late game. One mistake can result in instant death, so stay close to teammates and avoid walking alone. Focus on eliminating enemy damage dealers before team fights fully break out. Save your skills for priority targets and avoid wasting your combo on tanks unless necessary. Proper timing and patience are key to winning late game fights with Eudora.

emblems.emblem_desc:
Emblem

Mage Emblem

Talent Setup
- Inspire
- Bargain Hunter
- Lethal Ignition

This emblem setup is the most effective choice for revamped Eudora because it fully supports her burst damage and pick off playstyle. Inspire provides additional cooldown reduction, allowing Eudora to use her skill combo more frequently, which is crucial since her damage relies heavily on proper timing and rotations.

Bargain Hunter helps Eudora reach her core items faster by reducing item cost. Since Eudora is highly item dependent for burst damage, this talent accelerates her power spike and allows her to dominate earlier in the game, especially during mid game rotations and objective fights.

Lethal Ignition synergizes perfectly with Eudora's burst combo. After dealing damage with multiple skills, it triggers additional magic damage that enhances her ability to instantly eliminate squishy targets. This talent maximizes her assassination potential and makes her combos more punishing, especially against heroes with low magic defense.

Overall, this Mage Emblem setup strengthens Eudora's burst, improves her skill uptime, and ensures she remains a constant threat throughout the game.

equips.equip_desc:
Equipment

Core Build for Revamped Eudora

Arcane Boots
This item is essential for Eudora because it provides magic penetration early in the game. Since her strength lies in burst damage, magic penetration allows her skills to deal higher damage against enemy heroes, especially squishy targets in the early to mid game.

Clock of Destiny
Clock of Destiny gives Eudora scaling magic power, mana, and HP. This item fits well with her mid to late game power curve, allowing her burst to become more threatening over time while also giving slight survivability so she does not get instantly deleted in fights.

Lightning Truncheon
Lightning Truncheon synergizes perfectly with Eudora's combo based playstyle. After using skills, it releases additional magic damage that scales with mana, significantly boosting her burst potential. This item makes her combo deadlier and more reliable in deleting priority targets.

Holy Crystal
Holy Crystal greatly increases Eudora's magic power, amplifying all of her skill damage. This item is crucial in the mid to late game when enemies start building magic defense, as it ensures her burst remains lethal.

Divine Glaive
Divine Glaive provides percentage based magic penetration, making it extremely effective against heroes who stack magic defense. This allows Eudora to continue threatening tanks and fighters instead of becoming useless once enemies build resistance.

Blood Wings
Blood Wings gives a massive boost to magic power and provides a shield based on magic power. This item improves both damage and survivability, allowing Eudora to stay alive longer in late game team fights while maintaining extremely high burst damage.

Situational Item Options

Winter Truncheon
Best used against heavy dive or assassin compositions. The active effect allows Eudora to survive burst damage after engaging or being targeted.

hero.hero_overview:
Eudora is a magic damage mage in Mobile Legends: Bang Bang who has been recently revamped to make her more relevant, versatile, and impactful in the current meta. Her identity as a burst mage with strong crowd control remains central, but the revamp reworks her kit to improve skill synergy, combo potential, and teamfight presence. Rather than being just a one-target nuker, the revamped Eudora now offers area damage, enhanced stun utility, and stronger interactions with her passive, making her a bigger threat in both pick-offs and team engagements.

Eudora's gameplay revolves around the Superconductor mechanic, which works as both a mark and trigger for enhanced skill effects. Each of her abilities applies this mark, and hitting a marked enemy with subsequent skills causes additional effects, such as extra damage, area stun, or bouncing effects. Understanding this mechanic and properly timing skill combos is key to mastering Eudora, more so than her previous kit, which had more linear burst patterns.

While she remains a high-damage control mage, the revamp also shifts her toward being more teamfight-oriented. Her second skill now has stronger crowd control potential against groups of enemies, and her ultimate can deliver massive damage especially when targets are marked. These changes increase Eudora's effectiveness not only in straight burst kills but also in clustered engagements and multi-target situations.

Despite her power, Eudora still lacks mobility, meaning her positioning and timing are crucial. Her skills require her to be relatively close to her targets compared to some modern mages, and because she delivers damage in bursts and crowd control rather than sustained output, she must avoid getting caught out of position. Her strength lies in burst control, zoning, and skill accuracy rather than extended duels.
hero.hero_strength:
- Extremely high burst damage
Revamped Eudora excels at dealing massive magic burst damage, especially when her full combo is executed properly. With the Superconductor mechanic, she can instantly eliminate squishy heroes such as marksmen, mages, and assassins before they can react, making her a constant threat in mid and late game.

- Improved crowd control reliability
Her revamp enhances the consistency of her stun effects, allowing her to lock down enemies more effectively. This makes her not only strong for pick offs but also useful in small to medium scale team fights where control is crucial.

- Strong skill synergy through Superconductor
Eudora's skills now work better together due to her passive. Proper sequencing of abilities amplifies damage and control, rewarding players who understand timing and positioning rather than simple button pressing.

- High pick off potential
Eudora remains one of the best heroes for punishing poor positioning. Enemies who step into her range without proper vision can be instantly stunned and burst down, giving her team numerical advantage before objectives or fights.

- Effective zone control in mid lane
Her enhanced abilities allow her to control space more effectively in the mid lane. Enemies are forced to respect her range and stun threat, making it easier for her team to secure vision, rotate, or contest objectives.
hero.hero_weakness:
- Very limited mobility
Even after the revamp, Eudora still lacks any true escape or mobility skill. Once she is caught by divers or assassins, she has very limited options to reposition, making her heavily dependent on good positioning and team protection.

- Highly combo dependent
Eudora's damage output relies almost entirely on landing her full skill combo. Missing a key ability, especially her stun, drastically reduces her effectiveness and leaves her vulnerable during cooldowns.

- Long cooldown windows
After using her main skills, Eudora becomes significantly weaker while waiting for cooldowns. Enemies who survive her initial burst can easily punish her during this downtime.

- Weak against tanky and magic resistant heroes
Heroes with high magic defense, shields, or damage reduction can absorb Eudora's burst and render her less effective. Against these targets, she struggles to contribute meaningful damage without proper itemization or team follow up.

- Vulnerable to flanks and vision pressure
Because Eudora needs proper positioning to safely cast her skills, enemies who control vision or execute flanking plays can easily catch her off guard. This forces her to play more cautiously, especially in late game team fights.

data.recommend: Eudora is a high burst mage who excels at picking off squishy targets and controlling fights with her stun. She needs proper positioning and team support to avoid mobile assassins and dive heroes.

spell.spell_desc:
Battle Spell

Flicker

Flicker is the best and most consistent battle spell for revamped Eudora because it directly compensates for her biggest weakness, which is her lack of mobility. Since Eudora has no dash or escape skill, Flicker gives her a crucial repositioning tool that can save her from assassins, fighters, or sudden flank attempts during team fights.

Offensively, Flicker allows Eudora to perform unexpected engages. She can instantly close distance to land her stun and full burst combo on priority targets such as marksmen or mages. This surprise factor is extremely powerful, especially when enemies think they are safe due to distance or positioning.

Defensively, Flicker helps Eudora escape after casting her combo. Once her skills are on cooldown, she becomes very vulnerable, and Flicker gives her the ability to disengage, reposition behind allies, or cross walls to avoid being punished.

Overall, Flicker greatly increases Eudora's survivability, playmaking potential, and consistency across all stages of the game, making it the most reliable battle spell choice for the revamped version of the hero.

counters.counter_desc:

Star Rating: ★★★★★
 
Description: Hayabusa is a major threat to Eudora, as his high mobility and burst damage can quickly eliminate her before she can cast her skills. His 1st skill (Shadow Step) lets him close gaps instantly, while his ultimate (Ougi: Shadow Kill) allows him to dash through targets and avoid Eudora's skill shots. Hayabusa can also use bushes to ambush her—his mobility and evasive abilities make it hard for Eudora to land her stuns or slow effects. If Hayabusa gets within range, he can burst Eudora down with his combos before she can activate Flicker or rely on her team for protection. dominants.dominant_desc:
Dominant Phase
 
Early to Mid-Game Dominance Phase
 
Early to Mid-Game Dominance Phase
 
- Laning Precautions:
- Use your 1st skill (Electric Arrow) to harass enemies while clearing minion waves. Aim to bounce it to both minions and the enemy hero for extra pressure.
- Maintain a safe distance from bushes, as mobile assassins or roamers may target you. Use your 2nd skill (Thunderstruck) to check bushes by casting it into potential hiding spots.
- Manage mana carefully. Use basic attacks on minions when possible to save skill mana for harassing or escaping ganks.
- Position near your turret until you reach level 4. Once you unlock your ultimate (Judgment), you can be more aggressive and look for gank opportunities with your jungler.
- Team Fight Strategies:
- Wait for your allies to initiate before casting your ultimate. Place it in choke points or where enemies are likely to cluster to maximize area control and damage.
- Use Flicker to reposition if enemies try to focus you, or to adjust your ultimate's placement for better coverage.
- Chain your skills: start with 2nd skill to slow enemies, follow with 1st skill to stun, then activate your ultimate for sustained damage and crowd control.
- Prioritize squishy targets like marksmen or mages. Use your passive's stacked magic penetration to burst them down quickly before they can output damage.

emblems.emblem_desc:

This "Burst and Sustain Mage Setup" uses the Mage Emblem with the selected talent Agility (4% extra Movement Speed), complemented by other optimized talents for the revamped 2026 Eudora.
 
The 4% movement speed boost addresses her lingering mobility gap post-revamp, helping her reposition faster to stack her passive, land bouncing 1st skill hits, and adjust placement for her lingering 2nd skill and mobile ultimate. Combined with typical Mage Emblem attributes like magic power and cooldown reduction (from additional tier talents), this setup enhances both her burst potential and battlefield sustainability—allowing her to chase down enemies, dodge skill shots more effectively, and maintain pressure across all game phases. It synergizes perfectly with her revamped kit, which rewards precise positioning and consistent skill casting to maximize damage and crowd control effects.

- Conserve mana early; avoid spamming skills unnecessarily until you have mana regen items or blue buff.

- Always check bushes before moving through them, as Eudora is very squishy and vulnerable to early ganks.

- If the enemy mid-laner roams, ping missing and push your lane to gain a turret advantage.

- Team Fight Strategies (or Early Skirmishes):

- Look for opportunities to roam to side lanes (especially Gold Lane) at levels 2-4 once you have Skill 2 (Ball Lightning) and possibly your Ultimate (Thunder's Wrath).

- Hide in bushes near enemy laners. Wait for an enemy to overextend or be isolated.

- Execute your stun-burst combo (Skill 2 -> Skill 3 -> Skill 1) to secure quick kills and snowball your team.

- Coordinate with your jungler for ambushes; your stun is excellent for setting up ganks.

- Prioritize eliminating enemy marksmen or mages first.

hero.hero_overview:
Eudora's origins are rooted in a village where Light Elves faced prejudice despite their integration into Moniyan society. Born with unusual eyes and sharp ears, she was often bullied, leading to a withdrawn and sensitive personality. Her only solace was a silver song flower, which was cruelly destroyed by her tormentors. This act ignited a surge of pure rage, awakening her latent lightning magic and nearly leveling the surrounding homes.
 
This traumatic event and her emotional imbalance made controlling her magic difficult. Any insult to her appearance would cause her powers to spiral, forcing her family to constantly move. Their nomadic life ended when Theresa, a tutor from the Academy of Magic, recognized Eudora's talent and brought her to the school. Under Theresa's guidance, Eudora excelled, mastering lightning magic to an extent that surpassed her mentor. Driven by a desire to become the strongest mage, she sought challenges, engaging in duels with other talented mages like Valir and Harley. Her quest to master all magic led her to the Land of Dawn, a realm of warfare and witchcraft, where she aims to solidify her title as the most powerful sorceress.
 
Hero's Role in the Game
 
Eudora is a Mage hero known for her burst damage and crowd control. She excels at ambushing enemies and eliminating high-priority targets with a swift combination of skills. Her primary role is to:
 
- Mid Laner: She is typically played in the mid-lane, where she can quickly clear minion waves and gain experience and gold.

- Ganker: Eudora is a potent ganker due to her ability to stun and unleash massive damage, making her effective at roaming to other lanes and securing kills.

- Initiator/Pick-off: With her Ball Lightning, she can stun key enemies, allowing her team to follow up and eliminate them.

- Support (in some metas): While primarily a damage dealer, her strong crowd control can also provide valuable support in team fights.
hero.hero_strength:
Laning or Farming Advantages
 
- Efficient Minion Clear: Eudora's Skill 1, Forked Lightning, is excellent for clearing minion waves, especially when combined with her Superconductor passive. This allows her to quickly push lanes and gain gold and experience.
- Harassment: She can effectively poke and harass enemy laners with her Skill 1, forcing them to retreat or consume their healing items.
- Bush Control: Eudora excels at ambushing enemies from bushes. Her ability to instantly stun and burst down targets makes her a constant threat in the mid-lane.
 
Team Fight Advantages
 
- Devastating Burst Damage: Eudora is renowned for her ability to eliminate squishy enemy heroes (ADCs, Assassins, other Mages, Supports) almost instantly with her combo. If an enemy is caught by her full combo, especially when marked by Superconductor, it's often a guaranteed kill.
- Strong Crowd Control: Her Skill 2, Ball Lightning, provides a reliable stun. When a target is marked by Superconductor, this stun can bounce to nearby enemies, providing area-of-effect crowd control that can turn the tide of a team fight.
- Zone Control: Her presence alone can create fear among enemies, making them wary of engaging or moving through certain areas, especially near bushes.
- Objective Contestation: Her high burst damage makes her valuable for securing objectives like Lord or Turtle, as she can quickly melt down enemy heroes attempting to contest.
 
Early/Mid/Late Game Advantages
 
- Early Game:
- Strong Poke and Harass: Eudora can effectively pressure opponents in the laning phase with Skill 1.
- Early Kill Potential: Once she unlocks Skill 2 at level 2, she can perform a basic stun-and-damage combo. Upon reaching level 4 and unlocking her Ultimate, she gains immense kill potential, often capable of one-shotting many heroes if they are caught out of position.
- Gank Setup: Her stun makes her an excellent hero for setting up ganks with her jungler or other teammates.
hero.hero_weakness:
- Extremely Squishy (Low Durability):

- Eudora has very low HP and minimal defensive stats. She builds almost entirely magic damage items, leaving her highly vulnerable to any form of sustained damage or burst from assassins and marksmen.

- She cannot sustain herself in prolonged engagements and is easily killed if caught out of position.

- Lack of Mobility/Escape Skills:

- This is arguably her biggest weakness. Eudora has no dash, blink, or movement speed buff skills in her kit. This makes her incredibly reliant on proper positioning and the use of Battle Spells like Flicker or Sprint for either engagement or escape.

- If she misses her stun or her Flicker is on cooldown, she is an easy target for enemy ganks and dive compositions. This also makes her vulnerable to heroes that can quickly close the gap.

- Vulnerable to Crowd Control (CC) and Dive:

- Because of her low mobility and squishiness, heroes with strong CC (stuns, suppresses, silences) or those who can quickly dive into the backline (assassins, certain fighters) are very effective against her. If she gets locked down, she usually goes down instantly.

- She also has a slight delay on her Ultimate, which experienced enemies can use to their advantage to interrupt or escape.

Eudora's revamped Skill 1 can be fired into bushes to scout; if the lightning chain connects, it reveals their position and grants you the speed to reposition. The lock-on nature of her stun ensures they can't use their mobility to "dance" around your damage once revealed.

Healers rely on being near their allies to heal or provide buffs. Your revamped Skill 2 (Ball Lightning) will bounce between them, stunning the entire group. When you drop your Ultimate, the "Superconductor" marks will trigger multiple Thunderbursts simultaneously, turning their "healing circle" into a graveyard.

dominants.dominant_desc:
Your revamped Skill 1 (Forked Lightning) now creates a tether.
The Trick: If you suspect an enemy is in a bush, fire your S1 into it. If your Movement Speed suddenly increases, you have successfully "chained" a hidden enemy. Use this "Ghost Chain" to scout bushes without walking into them.

If the enemy Marksman is hiding behind a Tank, do not aim Skill 2 at the Marksman. Aim Skill 2 at the Tank. Because of the 2026 revamp, the stun will bounce off the Tank and hit the Marksman behind them, who likely didn't expect the stun to reach that far.

Keep a close eye on your gold. Because of Bargain Hunter, you will likely have your Wishing Lantern finished while the enemy Mid-laner is still 200 gold away from their second item. Force a fight at the Turtle immediately once your item finishes. This 2-minute "item lead" is where you win the game.

Do not dump your whole combo instantly if you see a shield. Use Skill 2 \rightarrow Skill 1 first. The "Chain" from S1 will tick away their shield. Wait 0.5 seconds for the Genius Wand stacks to hit their maximum (3 stacks), then drop the Ultimate. This ensures your big burst hits their actual HP, not the shield.

When a Fighter like Dyrroth or Martis rushes you, use S2 (Stun) \rightarrow S1 (Chain) and immediately run away. The speed boost acts like a "rubber band," pulling you out of their melee range while the chain continues to deal damage. Once they realize they can't catch you and turn to run, that is when you Ultimate their back.

emblems.emblem_desc: By utilizing the Thrill talent, you inject immediate raw power into her revamped Skill 1 "fan" attack, allowing you to dominate the lane and poke enemies into recall range without needing a full item. The inclusion of Bargain Hunter is the strategic pivot; it allows you to bypass the high cost of 2026 meta-items like Wishing Lantern and Genius Wand, ensuring you hit your "One-Shot" power spike before the enemy Roamer can farm enough gold for magic resistance. Finally, Lethal Ignition acts as the definitive executioner, adding a secondary burn to your high-frequency lightning chains that guarantees a kill even if the enemy survives the initial burst with a sliver of health.

equips.equip_desc:
This setup turns Eudora into an Economy-Bully. By using Bargain Hunter and Genius Wand, you effectively strip the enemy of their defense before they can afford it. You aren't just a mage; you are a tactical "breaker" that clears the way for your team to finish the job.

Divine Glaive is essential against enemies who have bought Athena's Shield. Without this, your damage will fall off a cliff in the late game.

hero.hero_overview:
Eudora is the ultimate answer to the game's fastest heroes. Her Superconductor passive ensures that her spells don't just hit—they evolve. Whether it's a stun that bounces between three enemies or a lightning strike that triggers a secondary explosion, she thrives when the enemy is grouped together.  

With the Overload Protocol build, she transcends the old "bush camper" stereotype. She is now a tactical powerhouse who can shred the magic defense of tanks and delete marksmen from the shadows. She is simple to pick up but offers immense depth in how you "chain" your lightning to control the flow of a battle.
hero.hero_strength: Her greatest strength lies in her "Overload" potential, where her Skill 1 (Forked Lightning) no longer just pokes but creates a lightning chain that grants her a movement speed boost, allowing her to kite and reposition with newfound agility. This mechanical shift is amplified by the "Overload Protocol" build, which swaps slow-scaling items for immediate Magic Penetration and the %HP shred of Wishing Lantern, turning her double-strike Ultimate into a nightmare for both squishy targets and high-HP tanks. By mastering the synergy of her bouncing Skill 2 stun and the armor-stripping effects of her passive, she becomes a team-wide threat capable of locking down and deleting entire enemy squads in a single, high-voltage chain reaction.
hero.hero_weakness: Eudora's primary downfall remains her lack of mobility and vulnerability to out-ranging. Despite her revamped chain effects, she must still put herself in harm's way to land her stun, making her a prime target for high-mobility assassins and long-range poke mages.

hero.hero_overview:
The revamped Eudora, the Lightning Weaver, officially arrived in Mobile Legends: Bang Bang on January 29, 2026. This update transforms her from a single-target "one-shot" assassin mage into a more versatile AoE burst mage with improved range and team-fight utility. 

Key Skill Adjustments
Passive (Superconductor): Retained her core mechanic where skills apply a "Superconductor" mark. Using subsequent skills on marked targets triggers enhanced effects.
Skill 1 (Forked Lightning): Optimized for higher damage and slightly increased range. It now features a second phase of damage if targets stay within range, significantly boosting her early-game poke.
Skill 2 (Ball Lightning): Primarily remains a reliable stun. While it can no longer bounce off minions as easily as before, it is still the primary tool for initiating her combo.
Ultimate (Thunder's Wrath): Shifted from a target-lock spell to an area-targeting ability. This allows her to hit multiple heroes more effectively, though it requires better aim. 

Gameplay & Build Overview
New Playstyle: She is now more effective in team fights where enemies are grouped. Players are encouraged to lead with the Ultimate or Skill 2 to apply Superconductor before following up with Skill 1 for maximum burst.

Core Build: Focuses on Magic Penetration and raw power.
Arcane Boots: For early penetration.
Genius Wand: To shred enemy magic defense.
Lightning Truncheon: Adds extra burst based on mana.
Sky Piercer: Often used for high-damage executions.
Strength/Weakness: She is considerably stronger in the early game and team fights but remains vulnerable to high-mobility assassins like Gusion or Ling who can dodge her combos.

Targeted CC: Her Skill 2 (Ball Lightning) offers reliable, lock-on stun, preventing enemy escape.

Superconductor Mechanic: Her combo (Skill 2 + 1/3) maximizes damage by reducing enemy magic defense.

hero.hero_overview:
Eudora is a burst magic damage mage who specializes in eliminating enemy heroes in a single combo. She is known for her powerful lightning based abilities and reliable crowd control. Her main role is to target and remove high priority enemies such as marksmen, assassins, and mages before team fights fully begin.
Eudora is commonly played in the mid lane where she focuses on clearing minion waves quickly and rotating to side lanes to assist teammates. Once she reaches level four and unlocks her ultimate ability, her kill potential increases significantly. Her second skill provides a guaranteed stun, allowing her to set up her full combo easily.
Her gameplay revolves around patience and positioning. She is most effective when hiding in bushes and waiting for the right opportunity to strike. When she successfully lands her stun followed by her ultimate and first skill, most squishy heroes cannot survive the damage.
Eudora does not rely on complicated mechanics. Her abilities are straightforward, making her beginner friendly while still rewarding good map awareness and timing. However, success with Eudora depends heavily on decision making and proper target selection.
During mid and late game, she plays as a pick off mage. Instead of starting long fights, she focuses on eliminating one key enemy to give her team a numbers advantage. With proper positioning and smart rotations, Eudora can control the pace of the game and create constant pressure across the map.
hero.hero_strength:
Eudora is one of the strongest burst mages in the game. Her biggest advantage is her ability to eliminate enemy heroes in a very short amount of time. Once she builds core magic items, her full combo can instantly defeat most marksmen, assassins, and mages. This makes her extremely dangerous during mid and late game.
Another major strength is her reliable crowd control. Her stun is simple to use and does not require perfect aim. Because of this, she can consistently lock down a target and follow up with her remaining skills. This reliability makes her very effective in both solo plays and coordinated team fights.
Eudora has strong early game pressure once she reaches level four. Her ultimate ability greatly increases her damage output. After unlocking it, she can rotate to side lanes and secure kills for her teammates. Early successful ganks allow her to snowball and gain a gold advantage.
She is also excellent at ambushing enemies. By hiding in bushes and waiting for the right moment, she can surprise opponents and eliminate them before they can react. This forces enemies to move carefully and avoid walking alone, which limits their map control.
Her skills are easy to understand, making her beginner friendly while still being effective in higher ranks. Players can focus more on positioning and decision making instead of complicated mechanics.
In team fights, removing one key damage dealer before the fight fully starts gives her team a strong advantage. This ability to quickly change the outcome of a fight makes Eudora a powerful and reliable burst mage throughout the match.
hero.hero_weakness:
Eudora has several clear weaknesses that players must understand. Her biggest weakness is the lack of mobility. She does not have any dash, blink, or escape skill. If she is caught out of position, she can be eliminated very quickly. Mobile assassins and fighters can easily dive into the backline and target her before she can react.
She is very dependent on her skill combo. Most of her damage comes from using all of her abilities together. After casting her full combo, her damage drops significantly while waiting for cooldowns. During this time, she cannot contribute much to the fight. If she fails to secure a kill with her burst, she becomes vulnerable and less threatening.
Eudora is also very fragile. She has low base health and defense compared to tanks and fighters. She cannot survive extended team fights or direct frontline engagements. If the enemy team focuses her, she can be defeated quickly.
Heroes who build magic defense items reduce her effectiveness. Tanks and durable fighters who stack resistance can survive her combo and continue fighting. This limits her ability to instantly remove key targets.
She is also weak against heavy crowd control. If she is stunned, silenced, or knocked up before casting her skills, she may be eliminated without dealing damage. Good positioning and awareness are critical for her survival.
Finally, she depends on vision and map awareness. Without proper information about enemy positions, her ambush playstyle becomes risky. When enemies group together and move carefully, it becomes harder for her to find isolated targets.
Eudora is powerful but unforgiving. One mistake in positioning can cost everything.

To Catch Up The Enemy Or To Escape When Someone Ambush You 
Flicker is a battle spell that instantly teleports your hero a short distance in the direction you choose. It allows you to quickly reposition during fights, escape dangerous situations, or chase enemies who are trying to run away. The teleport happens immediately after activation, making it one of the fastest and most reliable mobility tools in the game.
Flicker can be used both offensively and defensively. Defensively, it helps you escape from ganks, dodge enemy skills, or avoid fatal damage. Offensively, it can be used to close the gap between you and an enemy, surprise opponents, or secure a kill by repositioning for a better angle.
The spell ignores terrain, which means you can teleport through walls and obstacles. This makes it extremely valuable for escaping tight situations or setting up unexpected ambush plays. Timing is very important, because using Flicker too early or too late can result in failure.
For heroes like Eudora, Flicker is especially useful. Since she has no natural mobility, Flicker compensates for that weakness. It allows her to reposition after casting her combo or escape if enemies dive onto her. It can also be combined with her stun to surprise enemies from unexpected angles.
Flicker has a long cooldown, so it should be used carefully and not wasted. Smart players save it for critical moments where positioning determines survival or victory. Overall, Flicker is one of the most versatile and powerful battle spells available.
